# UC-21.3.3 · Nurse Call System Response Time

## Description

Nurse call response time is a key patient satisfaction and safety metric. Monitoring enables staffing optimization and regulatory compliance.

## Value

Service and risk leaders use signals from Nurse Call System Response Time to move crews, budget, and customer or regulator communications while the situation is still controllable, so the organization steers from live data rather than a post-incident report.

## Implementation

Integrate nurse call system events via syslog or API. Track response times by unit and shift. Alert when p95 exceeds 5 minutes. Report for CMS quality metrics. **Domain context:** Response time is a common patient experience and safety proxy; thresholds vary by acuity level (e.g. ICU vs med-surg)—segment by `unit` or priority if available. **Splunk:** Confirm whether `response_time` is seconds or milliseconds; normalize before `stats`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Nurse call system integration via syslog or HEC.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=healthcare` `sourcetype="nursecall:event"` fields `call_id`, `call_time`, `response_time`, `unit`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Integrate nurse call system events via syslog or API. Track response times by unit and shift. Alert when p95 exceeds 5 minutes. Report for CMS quality metrics. **Domain context:** Response time is a common patient experience and safety proxy; thresholds vary by acuity level (e.g. ICU vs med-surg)—segment by `unit` or priority if available. **Splunk:** Confirm whether `response_time` is seconds or milliseconds; normalize before `stats`.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=healthcare sourcetype="nursecall:event"
| eval response_sec=response_time
| bin _time span=1h
| stats avg(response_sec) as avg_response, perc95(response_sec) as p95_response, count by unit, _time
| where p95_response > 300
| table _time, unit, avg_response, p95_response, count
```

Understanding this SPL

**Nurse Call System Response Time** — Nurse call response time is a key patient satisfaction and safety metric. Monitoring enables staffing optimization and regulatory compliance.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=healthcare` `sourcetype="nursecall:event"` fields `call_id`, `call_time`, `response_time`, `unit`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Nurse call system integration via syslog or HEC. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: healthcare; **sourcetype**: nursecall:event. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=healthcare, sourcetype="nursecall:event". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **response_sec** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Discretizes time or numeric ranges with `bin`/`bucket`.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by unit, _time** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Filters the current rows with `where p95_response > 300` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Pipeline stage (see **Nurse Call System Response Time**): table _time, unit, avg_response, p95_response, count


Step 3 — Validate
Compare Splunk results with the EHR, LIS, pharmacy, or device vendor view and spot-check fields against the Epic/Cerner (or equivalent) audit export for the same user, patient context, or device and time range.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Bar chart (avg response by unit), Line chart (trend over shifts), Table (units exceeding threshold).

## SPL

```spl
index=healthcare sourcetype="nursecall:event"
| eval response_sec=response_time
| bin _time span=1h
| stats avg(response_sec) as avg_response, perc95(response_sec) as p95_response, count by unit, _time
| where p95_response > 300
| table _time, unit, avg_response, p95_response, count
```

## Visualization

Bar chart (avg response by unit), Line chart (trend over shifts), Table (units exceeding threshold).
